figures.caption: Fig. 2: AnySlot overview. We formulate slot-level placement as goal-conditioned control. High-level goal construction uses the Nano-Banana image generator to render a blue-sphere goal from the language prompt, lifting it to a view-consistent multi-view overlay via depth and camera calibration. Low-level control uses a goal-conditioned VLA policy (π0.5) with a PaliGemma-3B backbone and action expert, mapping goalaugmented multi-view observations to continuous actions At.
Fig. 1: Overview of flat (a), modular (b), and (c) our goal-conditioned policy.
Fig. 4: Comparison between AnySlot and a VLM-based method. AnySlot accurately grounds the target slot and executes successful placement, while the VLM-based method mislocalizes the target and fails.

Table 2: Ablation on high-level grounding module. Report Coarse Accuracy (CA, %), Instruction Accuracy (IA, %) and Latency (s). † means inference performed on a single H200 GPU. The yellow rows denote the module used by AnySlot.

Table 1: Performance comparison of different methods on the SlotBench benchmark. Each entry reports SR (Success Rate; higher is better, %) and IA (Instruction Accuracy; higher is better, %). The yellow rows denote our proposed AnySlot, while the blue rows indicate the oracle upper bound.
Fig. 3: SlotBench. A slot-level benchmark with nine challenges for spatial reasoning and language grounding in manipulation.
